name: Nintendo
description: The Japanese gaming giant's consoles, games, and history

questions.q: Nintendo was originally founded in what year?

source.notes: Founded by Fusajiro Yamauchi as a playing card company. Still headquartered in Kyoto.

questions.q: Before becoming a video game company, what did Nintendo originally manufacture?

source.notes: Traditional Japanese playing cards. Nintendo still makes them today.

questions.q: Approximately what does the name 'Nintendo' mean in Japanese?

source.notes: Common translation of '任天堂' (nin-ten-dō). Though the interpretation is debated.

questions.q: Which 1981 arcade game gave Nintendo its first major international hit?

source.notes: Designed by Shigeru Miyamoto. Featured 'Jumpman' (later Mario).

questions.q: In what year was the NES (Nintendo Entertainment System) released in North America?

source.notes: Test-launched in NYC October 1985. Full North American release 1986. Revived the US video game market.

questions.q: What year was the original Game Boy released?

source.notes: Released in Japan April 1989, US July 1989. Tetris made it a cultural phenomenon.

questions.q: When was the Nintendo 64 released in North America?

source.notes: September 1996 in North America. Launched with 'Super Mario 64' and 'Pilotwings 64.'

questions.q: Which Nintendo console was released with an innovative trackpad controller in 1990?

source.notes: SNES released in Japan Nov 1990, US 1991. The SNES controller introduced the 'shoulder buttons.'

questions.q: What Nintendo console competed with the Sega Genesis in the late '80s and early '90s?

source.notes: Generally considered the 'console wars' of the 4th generation.

questions.q: Who designed Donkey Kong, Mario, and The Legend of Zelda?

source.notes: Joined Nintendo in 1977. Widely regarded as one of the greatest video game designers.

questions.q: Which Nintendo handheld was released in 2004 with dual screens?

source.notes: November 2004 in Japan. 'DS' stands for 'Dual Screen' or 'Developers' System.'

questions.q: Which Nintendo console was released in 2006 with motion-controlled 'Wii Remotes'?

source.notes: Named Wii for its accessibility to 'we' (everyone). Sold ~102M units.

questions.q: What hybrid console released by Nintendo in 2017 works both portably and docked to a TV?

source.notes: Released March 3, 2017. Over 140M units sold — one of best-selling consoles ever.

questions.q: What was the first Pokémon video game called (released 1996 in Japan)?

source.notes: Red/Green in Japan, Red/Blue in North America (1998).

questions.q: What was Nintendo's failed 1995 attempt at virtual reality gaming?

source.notes: Discontinued after less than a year. Red-and-black stereoscopic display caused headaches.

questions.q: What city is Nintendo headquartered in?

source.notes: Headquartered there since founding in 1889.

questions.q: Which Nintendo game franchise features the Hylian hero Link?

source.notes: First game released 1986. Princess Zelda is the series' namesake, but Link is the protagonist.

questions.q: Which Nintendo franchise features a pink puffball who can inhale enemies?

source.notes: First game 'Kirby's Dream Land' (1992) on Game Boy. Created by Masahiro Sakurai.

questions.q: What is the name of Nintendo's bounty hunter in a power suit, protagonist of a long-running sci-fi franchise?

source.notes: Debuted 1986 in 'Metroid.' Her being female was a famous reveal at game's end.

questions.q: What Nintendo game pits characters from many Nintendo franchises in a battle royale?

source.notes: First released 1999 on N64. Created by Masahiro Sakurai.
